task.title: Local syntax check of a remote file with the OS/390 CICS Transaction Server syntax
task.shortdesc: You can request a local syntax check of a remote file with the OS/390 CICS Transaction Server syntax.

keywords.indexterm: local syntax checkremote file with OS/390 CICS Transaction Server syntax

taskbody.prereq: To check the syntax of a remote CICS COBOL or PL/I program with the OS/390 CICS Transaction Server syntax, CICS TX Series version 6.1 or above must be installed on your workstation.

context.p: The COBOL or PL/I compiler calls the CICS Translator on your behalf if the CICS('CTSxx') (for COBOL) or pp(CICS('CTSxx') (for PL/I) compiler option is specified as part of your local compiler options. This compiler option is automatically generated on a local syntax check of a remote CICS COBOL file if the CICS (contains EXEC CICS statements) checkbox is selected on the Runtime Environments tab of the COBOL or PL/I Settings properties page for the remote file. On this page, you can select the version of CICS Transaction Server that is installed on the remote system.

Because the CICS('CTSxx') (for COBOL) or pp(CICS('CTSxx') (for PL/I) compiler options are only intended for syntax checking purposes, you cannot use them when you build a DLL that runs in a CICS region. If this option is specified in the IDE for any action other than a syntax check, an error window opens and the build ends.
